title: How the Digital SAT Scoring Works (Adaptive Sections Explained)
description: Understand how the Digital SAT's adaptive scoring system works, what the two modules mean, and how to maximize your score.
author: Lyceon Team
content:
The Digital SAT uses a two-module adaptive system that adjusts difficulty based on your performance. Here's exactly how it works and what it means for your strategy.

## How Adaptive Testing Works

Both the Reading & Writing and Math sections have two modules each. Everyone takes the same Module 1. Based on your performance, you're routed to either an easier or harder Module 2.

**Higher difficulty = higher score ceiling.** If you're routed to the harder Module 2, you have access to the full 800-point scale for that section. The easier Module 2 has a lower maximum score.

## The Scoring Math

Your raw score (questions correct) is converted to a scaled score of 200-800 for each section. The conversion depends on which Module 2 you received and the overall difficulty of your specific test form.

Final score range: 400-1600 (same as the paper SAT).

## What This Means for Strategy

1. **Module 1 matters most.** A strong start gets you the harder Module 2 and access to top scores.
2. **Don't stress if Module 2 feels hard.** That's a good sign—it means you did well on Module 1.
3. **Every question counts equally.** There's no penalty for guessing, so answer every question.

## Practical Tips

- Practice with adaptive-style questions to get comfortable with varying difficulty
- Focus on accuracy in the first half of each section
- Use all available time—rushing leads to careless errors

Ready to practice? Try our [adaptive SAT practice](/digital-sat) with instant feedback on every question.
